title: thomsonaria fragrans
intro: Thomsonaria fragrans, also known as the sweet tree of life, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae that is native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which have been used to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Thomsonaria fragrans include reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Thomsonaria fragrans is typically prepared as a decoction, where the leaves are steeped in hot water to create a tea that can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
